citation: Okada, T. (2024). LSHM: TTRPG To The Bitter End. Google Docs.
summary:
This short Low Stress High Mayhem resource document supports a streamed session of Takuma Okada's *To the Bitter End!* It identifies the game, links to the Twitch VOD, points to Okada's social and itch.io pages, and notes that the game appears in *The Ultimate Micro-RPG Book*, edited by James D'Amato. The handout then gathers play-alikes, safety tools, GameRT links, a Twitch chat policy, and a brief stream outline.

The play-alike section frames *To the Bitter End!* around "versus vibes," with recommendations such as *Halo 'n' Horns*, *First Dig Two Graves*, *Swords as Subtext*, *High Noon*, *A Duel with Shadows*, and *Gunslingin' in Space*. The safety section points viewers toward *Script Change*, *The Door Is Open*, and the TTRPG Safety Toolkit. The Twitch policy emphasizes kindness, respect, no hate speech, moderator intervention, harassment reporting, and private communication options.

The stream outline is the most program-relevant part of the document. It lays out a practical sequence: tech check, introduction and rules, character creation, explanation of play flow, actual play, emotional check-in, discussion of rules and playtesting, favorite moments, programming potential, safety-tool considerations, and chat questions. This makes the item useful as a trace of how GameRT translated a small TTRPG into a facilitated online event.

For the larger GBLS project, the document may be useful as a practitioner example of game advisory and program scaffolding. It shows how a streamed tabletop program can combine creator attribution, discovery pathways, play-alike recommendations, safety resources, community moderation policy, and a structured post-play discussion. These elements could inform practical guidance for libraries designing low-preparation TTRPG streams or online game demonstrations.

However, this source should remain outside the main literature review. It is not scholarship, does not report a study, and does not describe a library program in enough detail to support claims about GBLS outcomes or implementation. Its best use is as an excluded primary artifact that may later support an appendix on advisory metadata, safety tooling, or streamed game-program planning.
sourceText:
The Game
To the Bitter End! By Takuma Okada
Watch the VOD of this session here: https://www.twitch.tv/collections/tpuBAP2vGhcF7A
Takuma Okada’s Pages & Games https://twitter.com/takuma_okada_ https://noroadhome.itch.io/
Knife to your Throat Alone on a Journey
Found in: The Ultimate Micro-RPG Book edited by James D’amato https://www.simonandschuster.com/books/The-Ultimate-Micro-RPG-Book/James-D-Amato/TheUltimate-RPG-Guide-Series/9781507212868
James D’amato’s Pages https://twitter.com/OneShotRPG http://oneshotpodcast.com/
Play Alikes
Versus Vibes ● ● Halo ‘n’ Horns byconniechang ● First Dig Two Graves by A Couple of Drakes ● Swords as Subtext by boyproblems ● High Noon by Helberon Publishing ● A Duel with Shadows by Color Spray Games ● Gunslingin’ In Space by hannah j. gray
Extra Resources ● Safety Tools ○ Script Change by Beau Jágr Sheldon ○ The Door is Open by Eirik Fatland ■ Blog by Lizzie Stark ■ Blog 2 by Eirik Fatland ○ TTRPG Safety Toolkit collected by Kienna Shaw and Lauren Bryant-Monk ■ TTRPG Safety Toolkit Guide ■ Google Drive

Twitch Policy
1. Be kind and respectful to one another 2. Hate speech of any type will not be tolerated 3. We reserve the right to remove anyone from the chat for any reason 4. If you are feeling harassed by anyone, please notify a GameRT twitch mod and one of us will assist you 5. If you'd rather talk privately about anything, please send us a direct message
Learn More About GameRT Blog: bit.ly/GameRTBlog Facebook: bit.ly/GameRTLeague & bit.ly/GameRTFacebook Resources: bit.ly/GameRTPublicResources Website: bit.ly/GameRTWebsite Questions? Email gamert@ala.org
Stream Summary
Tech Check on Twitch then Go Time!
Part 1: Intro and Rules ● Who are we & what are we doing??? ● Breakdown of Stream ● Breakdown of Game ○ Character Creation ○ Flow of Play ● Segue: Ready to play?
Part 2: Actual Play ● READY…FIGHT! ● Segue: How do you Feel?
Part 3: Discussion ● Figuring out Rules & Playtesting ● Parts We Really Enjoyed ● Programing Potential ● Safety Tools Considerations ● Chat Questions & Comments ● Segue: That’s all we’ve got for this session…
Ending ● Come back again for our cool things!!

● Thank you so much for joinings for ● Russell: Low Stress and ● Danielle: High Mayhem ● Bye!!
